---
title: "MED-187: Interventional decomposition of phoneme blending via letter-sounds"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-note}
Drafted by a LLM-based AI tool (Codex/GPT-5).
:::

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

MED-187 is the parent+100 **interventional-effects companion** to [MED-087](../lrp-rli-med-087/). Both fit the same Phase-0 joint model, use letter-sound knowledge at t2 as the mediator, use the same signed-off adjustment set, and model the graded phoneme-blending score at t2. MED-087 reports the natural direct and indirect effects (NDE/NIE); MED-187 reports their randomised-interventional analogues (IDE/IIE) on the blending-item scale.

## Why the estimand changes

Sessions attended ($IS$) are affected by randomised group and are a common cause of letter sounds and blending through $IS\to L$ and $IS\to B$. This recanting-witness structure makes the cross-world natural effects formally unidentified. The interventional analogue instead draws the mediator from its arm-specific covariate-conditional distribution, so it remains well-defined without asking for the same child's mediator under incompatible treatment worlds.

That change does **not** make MED-187 a fully identified causal mediation analysis. Latent general ability still confounds the $L\to B$ relationship, mediator and outcome are measured in the same wave, and $IS$ is not represented in the fitted joint model. IDE/IIE are therefore weaker-assumption, model-based decompositions under the stated assumptions, not identified causal routes.

## Comparison with MED-087

The fitted probabilistic model and adjustment set are intentionally identical to MED-087. In the current fully parametric g-formula, both estimand classes draw $L$ from the same fitted covariate-conditional law; changing `estimand` changes the counterfactual interpretation and the row labels, not the numerical functional. MED-187's IIE should therefore agree with MED-087's NIE up to posterior-sampling and g-formula Monte Carlo error. Agreement is an implementation check; it is **not** evidence that shared dose causes no distortion. A material gap would first indicate non-comparable fits or Monte Carlo error, not a scientific dose-confounding result.

## Adjustment set

The set is inherited directly from MED-087: randomised group $G$, age $A$, baseline letter sounds $L_{t1}$, the outcome's own baseline $B_{t1}$, hearing $HS$ and speech production $SP$, with the raw-covariate missing indicators. Phonological memory is not a common cause of letter sounds and blending and is excluded. The time-indexed derivation in `notes/202607142340-lrp264-mediation-adjustment-dsep.md` confirms that baseline expressive/receptive vocabulary is not required and that no treatment-non-descendant adjustment set can block the $L_2\leftarrow IS_1\to PA_2$ witness path.

{{< include _partials/_results_mediation.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
